# Top 50 WAF documents by SEO value

Ranked by estimated search traffic value — documents covering topics users actively search for on Google.

**Generated:** 2026-03-31
**Documents scanned:** 119

**Scoring factors:** Topic search volume relevance (title match weighted 3x, description 2x, body frequency), content depth (word count), code examples (implementers search for these). Index pages are penalized as they are less targeted.

## How to use this list

1. **High-scoring documents deserve investment.** These cover topics users actively search for. Ensure they are comprehensive, well-structured, and have strong SEO metadata.
2. **Low word counts on high-SEO topics are opportunities.** A document ranking high on SEO value but with thin content (< 700 words) has the most to gain from expansion.
3. **Cross-reference with the WAF alignment report.** A high-SEO, low-WAF document should be prioritized — it will attract traffic but may not retain users if the content quality is poor.
